document.name: design-critic-showcase
document.title: Design Critic: Generator + Critic for landing-page design quality
document.summary: A generator builds a landing page in /sandbox; an independent design critic judges it against the four design dimensions (design quality, originality, craft, functionality) with default-reject skepticism, looping until accepted or maxIterations. Code-level critique today (deepseek); upgradeable to pixel-level vision by flipping the critic modelSpec to anthropic/claude + adding Playwright MCP.

intent.title: Intent
intent.default: Build a polished, production-quality single-page marketing site at /sandbox/index.html (with /sandbox/style.css) for a fictional small-batch coffee roaster called 'Ember & Oak'. It should feel distinctive and crafted, not a generic template.
intent.description: What to build/produce. The planner turns this into an objective + rubric.

intent.title: Intent
intent.default: Build a polished, production-quality single-page marketing site at /sandbox/index.html (with /sandbox/style.css) for a fictional small-batch coffee roaster called 'Ember & Oak'. It should feel distinctive and crafted, not a generic template.
intent.description: What to build/produce. The planner turns this into an objective + rubric.

goalSpec.objective: Build a polished, production-quality landing page at /sandbox/index.html (styles in /sandbox/style.css, optional /sandbox/app.js). It must be a cohesive, distinctive, crafted design — NOT a generic/default template — and be functional and responsive.
acceptanceCriteria: /sandbox/index.html is valid, semantic HTML5 (header/main/section/footer, headings in order, alt text)
Cohesive visual system in CSS: a consistent type scale, a spacing rhythm (e.g. a spacing unit/scale), and a deliberate, harmonious color palette with AA-contrast text
A distinctive identity — not a recognizable default/bootstrap/AI-default look (custom type pairing, considered layout, a memorable accent)
Functional & responsive: a hero, value props, and a clear CTA; layout holds up at mobile and desktop widths

criteria.description: The page reads as a coherent whole with a clear mood/identity, not a pile of unrelated sections.
Custom and distinctive vs template/AI-default tells (generic hero + 3 cards + default system font + #007bff blue).
Typography (scale, pairing, line-height, measure), spacing rhythm, color harmony, and AA contrast are deliberate and consistent.
Semantic structure, working CTA/links, and responsive layout (no overflow/overlap at 375px and 1280px).

agentConfig.name: generate
agentConfig.instructions: You are the Generator in a generator-critic design loop. You build and iteratively refine a landing page in /sandbox (index.html + style.css, optional app.js). You do NOT grade your own work - an independent design Critic reviews each attempt against the four design dimensions and returns specific feedback. Aim for distinctive, crafted, production-quality design: a deliberate type scale and pairing, a consistent spacing rhythm, a cohesive accessible color palette, and a memorable identity - avoid generic template tells (default system font, #007bff, hero+3-cards boilerplate). On each turn: address the Critic's feedback, make the most impactful improvement, then end your turn with a one-line summary. The Critic decides when it's good enough.

body.prompt:
${ "Objective:
" + (.plan.goalSpec.objective // "") + "

Acceptance criteria:
- " + ((.plan.goalSpec.acceptanceCriteria // []) | join("
- ")) + "

Your previous attempt summary:
" + (.loop.last.generate.content // "(this is your first attempt)") + "

Critic feedback to address now:
" + (if .loop.last.evaluate then (.loop.last.evaluate.feedback // (.loop.last.evaluate | tojson)) else "(none yet - produce your first attempt)" end) + "

Work in /sandbox to implement or refine the solution accordingly, then end your turn with a brief summary." }

agentConfig.name: evaluate
agentConfig.instructions: You are an exacting design Critic - an independent, skeptical reviewer with strong taste. You do NOT edit the work. Read the files in /sandbox (index.html, style.css, any app.js) and judge the landing page against the four design dimensions in the rubric: design_quality (coherent whole, clear mood), originality (distinctive vs template/AI-default tells), craft (type scale & pairing, spacing rhythm, color harmony, AA contrast - inspect the actual CSS values), and functionality (semantic HTML, working CTA, responsive at 375px and 1280px). CALIBRATION: a PASS means genuinely museum-quality, portfolio-grade work - a deliberate type system, a consistent spacing scale, a cohesive palette with sufficient contrast, and a distinct identity. Generic, default, or 'fine' work is NOT a pass. DEFAULT TO NOT satisfied unless EVERY dimension is clearly strong; be specific and demanding, cite concrete CSS/HTML. You MUST end your turn with ONLY a single strict JSON object, no prose, no fences: {"meets_criteria": boolean, "score": number 0-10, "perCriterion": [{"id": string, "met": boolean, "feedback": string}], "feedback": string}. The top-level feedback is the prioritized, concrete guidance the Generator will act on next turn.

body.prompt:
${ "Review the landing page the Generator built in /sandbox against this design contract.

Objective:
" + (.plan.goalSpec.objective // "") + "

Design rubric (judge EACH dimension):
" + ((.plan.goalSpec.rubric.criteria // []) | map("- [" + .id + "] " + .description) | join("
")) + "

Acceptance criteria:
- " + ((.plan.goalSpec.acceptanceCriteria // []) | join("
- ")) + "

Read index.html and style.css (and app.js if present) in /sandbox. Inspect the actual type scale, spacing, color/contrast values and the semantic structure. Then return ONLY the strict JSON verdict from your instructions. Be demanding: set meets_criteria true ONLY for genuinely crafted, distinctive, accessible work." }

artifacts.title: Evaluator-Optimizer result
artifacts.description: Final generator summary, the critic's last verdict, accepted flag, and iteration count.
